pass_condition: apex serves 200 or a clean 301/308 to www that lands 200
observed: https://noetfield.com/ returned HTTP 308 with Location: https://www.noetfield.com/ (single hop), and the follow landed HTTP 200 text/html at https://www.noetfield.com/. Direct probe of https://www.noetfield.com/ returned HTTP 200. Apex 404 is no longer reproducible.

probes.note: reached by following the hop-1 308; an HTTP/2 103 early-hints interim response preceded the 200 (informational, not a hop)
independent comparison probe of www directly (separate curl invocation)

commands: curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w 'final_code=%{http_code} final_url=%{url_effective} num_redirects=%{num_redirects}' https://noetfield.com/  -> final_code=200 final_url=https://www.noetfield.com/ num_redirects=1
evidence.body_check: GET https://noetfield.com/ (followed) returned an HTML document starting '<!DOCTYPE html>' with title 'Noetfield Systems Inc. — AI Motors & Governed Executio...' — a real page, not an error body
evidence.serving_worker_header: x-noetfield-proxy: cf-www-proxy on every hop, confirming the proxy worker with apex-redirect logic is the responder

notes: P1 finding 'apex 404' from noos-nf-pub-route-inventory-v1 is CLOSED: the apex now issues a clean single-hop 308 to www which lands 200. 103 responses seen in curl -I output are HTTP early hints, not redirect hops.
